articles.title: Starbucks Korea Is Seeing Sales Drop After A Controversial 'Tank Day' Marketing Campaign Sparked Politica
articles.summary: Starbucks Korea is experiencing a significant sales decline due to a controversial "Tank Day" marketing campaign that coincided with the anniversary of the May 18 Gwangju Uprising. The campaign, which invoked a politically sensitive event, led to public outrage and an apology from Shinsegae Group chairman Chung Yong-jin, who operates Starbucks Korea. This incident occurs as Starbucks global implements a restructuring plan, including job cuts and office closures, with analysts like TD Cowen maintaining a positive outlook on the company's turnaround efforts despite the Korean unit's misstep.
